title: Egyptian Chicken Tagine
description: A traditional North African stew originating from Morocco, made with chicken, spices, and dried fruits. This dish is slow-cooked in a clay pot with a conical lid, allowing the flavors to meld together.
instructions: Heat the garlicky olive oil in a large clay pot over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides.
Add the egyptian cinnamon, sherry vinegar, and tagine spice mix.
Pour in the chicken broth and bring to a boil.
Cover the pot with a conical lid and transfer to the oven.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Serve the chicken and sauce over couscous or rice.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
